If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 227,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjMwNDY0MjI3\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/30464227?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/HarveyLink\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/HarveyLink/subscriptions\"}],\"node_id\":\"I_kwDOACyxMM6OYKuo\"},{\"id\":1946063219,\"number\":39303,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":true,\"title\":\"[BUG] NetworkSecurityGroup DesintationPortRange cannot support multiple ports anymore\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nAzure.ResourceManager.Network 1.5.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nIt used to be that with Fluent, you could specify multiple ports to allow as the \\\"`ToPortRanges()`\\\" method in an NSG:\\r\\n```\\r\\nnsg.Update()\\r\\n    .DefineRule(TestConstants.RuleName)\\r\\n        .AllowInbound()\\r\\n        .FromAnyAddress()\\r\\n        .FromAnyPort()\\r\\n        .ToAnyAddress()\\r\\n        .ToPortRanges(new string[] { TestConstants.ServiceFabricClientPort.ToString(), TestConstants.ServiceFabricWebPort.ToString(), TestConstants.DNCPort.ToString(), TestConstants.CNSPort.ToString(), EnvironmentVariables[TestConstants.CustomerPortEnvVarName] })\\r\\n        .WithProtocol(SecurityRuleProtocol.Tcp)\\r\\n        .WithDescription(TestConstants.RuleDescription)\\r\\n        .WithPriority(TestConstants.RulePriority)\\r\\n        .Attach()\\r\\n    .Apply();\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nNow however, this is not possible with ArmClient:\\r\\n\\r\\n```\\r\\nSecurityRuleData newRule = new SecurityRuleData();\\r\\nnewRule.Name = TestConstants.RuleName;\\r\\nnewRule.Description = TestConstants.RuleDescription;\\r\\nnewRule.Access = SecurityRuleAccess.Allow;\\r\\nnewRule.Direction = SecurityRuleDirection.Inbound;\\r\\nnewRule.SourceAddressPrefix = \\\"*\\\";\\r\\nnewRule.SourcePortRange = \\\"*\\\";\\r\\nnewRule.DestinationAddressPrefix = \\\"*\\\";\\r\\nnewRule.DestinationPortRange = TestConstants.ServiceFabricClientPort.ToString() + \\\",\\\" + TestConstants.ServiceFabricWebPort.ToString() + \\\",\\\" + TestConstants.DNCPort.ToString() + \\\",\\\" + TestConstants.CNSPort.ToString() + \\\",\\\" + EnvironmentVariables[TestConstants.CustomerPortEnvVarName];\\r\\nnewRule.Protocol = SecurityRuleProtocol.Tcp;\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nThis now throws an error:\\r\\n```\\r\\nresourceGroup.GetNetworkSecurityGroups().CreateOrUpdate(WaitUntil.Completed, nsgResourceID.Name, nsg.Data);\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\n```\\r\\nAzure.RequestFailedException: 'Security rule has invalid Port range. Value provided: 19000,19080,9001,10090,8081. Value should be an integer OR integer range with '-' delimiter. Valid range 0-65535.\\r\\nStatus: 400 (Bad Request)\\r\\nErrorCode: SecurityRuleInvalidPortRange\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nUnless I am missing something on how to allow multiple, specified ports, I think this is a regression from Fluent to ArmClient\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nWould like to be able to set multiple specific ports on an NSG rule using ArmClient\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nError saying:\\r\\n\\\"Azure.RequestFailedException: 'Security rule has invalid Port range. Value provided: 19000,19080,9001,10090,8081. Value should be an integer OR integer range with '-' delimiter. Valid range 0-65535.\\r\\nStatus: 400 (Bad Request)\\r\\nErrorCode: SecurityRuleInvalidPortRange\\\"\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\nSee description\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n_No Task\\u003cint\\u003e AddPublicIpAddressesAsync(ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup, ApplicationGatewayData appdata)\\r\\n {\\r\\n    PublicIPAddressCollection ipCollection =  catch (Exception e)\\r\\n        {\\r\\n            Console.WriteLine(e.Message);\\r\\n        }\\r\\n\\r\\n        return 0;\\r\\n    }\\r\\n```\\r\\nIf I don't call the code snippet then the add of the gateway returns that a FrontendConfiguration is required.\\r\\nCalling the code above the adding to the PublicIPAddressCollection works, and I see it in the portal.\\r\\nIf I don't add any HttpListeners it returns that at least one listener is required.\\r\\nWhen I add the listener then it returns that FrontendIPConfigurationId of the listener is invalid, which it is because it is null.\\r\\nSince this is not a separate collection how do I get a valid FrontendIPConfigurationId?\\r\\n\\r\\n### Expected behavior\\r\\n\\r\\nI should be able to add a
